summary: Back at a World Cup at last — likely a 3-5-2 built on wide play, set-piece strength and big-moment goalscorers, defending in a mid-block. Come on Scotland.
style: Likely 3-5-2 — lots of wide play & crosses, releasing wing-backs (Robertson/Hickey), underlapping/overlapping from an attacking LCB (Tierney), mid-block then force wide and defend the box.

counterReliance.note: Mid-block, force wide and defend the box

strengths: Dangerous wing-backs and an overlapping LCB in Tierney

keyPlayers.name: Ben Doak
keyPlayers.note: Pace to burn, fearless two-footed dribbler with an ever-improving final ball — potential breakout star

tactics: Defend in a mid-block, force play wide and protect the box, then break through wide overloads and crosses with set-plays as a key weapon. Can escape Group C but must be near-perfect, starting against an unpredictable Haiti.
sources: John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
